etrum, A Possible Future for Kepler and Ptolemy II". 3rd Workshop on Advances in the Kepler Scientific Workflow System and Its Applications, ICCS, 9, June, 2016; Poster presented at ICCS 2016 , San Diego. Abstract Triquetrum is an open platform for

iego. Abstract Triquetrum is an open platform for managing and executing scientific workflows that is under development as an Eclipse project. Both Triquetrum and Kepler use Ptolemy II as their execution engine. Triquetrum presents opportun

execution engine. Triquetrum presents opportunities and risks for the Kepler community. The opportunities include a possibly larger community for interaction and a path for Kepler to move from Kepler's one-off ant-based build environmen

rom Kepler's one-off ant-based build environment towards a more common Open Services Gateway initiative (OSGi)-based environment and a way to maintain a stable Ptolemy II core. The risks include the fact that Triquetrum is a fork of Ptolem

clude the fact that Triquetrum is a fork of Ptolemy II that would result in package name changes and other possible changes. In addition, Triquetrum is licensed under the Eclipse Public License v1.0, which includes a patent clause that coul

v1.0, which includes a patent clause that could conflict with the University of California patent clause. This paper describes these opportunities and risks. Electronic downloads TriquetrumICCS_CameraReady.pdf · application/pdf · 1287 kbyt
